system.docHtml: A split length of oak or ash, thickly wrapped with pitch-soaked rags and bound with twine, carried alight to illuminate passages and courtyards. Torches burn bright and hot, casting sharp shadows; a guard holding a torch can see far but is himself seen clearly. They are cheap, expendable, and commonly carried by watch-wardens, miners, and anyone entering a lightless space for a short time.
